web.markdown:
# eSignature API for Fast, Secure, and Legally Binding Document Signing

Integrate legally binding, SOC 2-compliant eSignatures into your app in less than a day. No monthly minimums, no seat limits, full security.


[Get Started](https://www.signwell.com/app/api-upgrade?free_api=true) [Read API Documentation](https://developers.signwell.com/)

## 65,000+ BUSINESSES

use SignWell for easy electronic signatures


![Sumo](https://www.signwell.com/assets/logos/sumo-logo-4b2df90057566c62be530bb608ecf48f3377b7250793a2884da82646a3222c73.png)![TinySeed](https://www.signwell.com/assets/logos/tinyseed-logo-2c1849bf53e5d269b9e5b7868f9d4d589288d8d2da5216f06715bb56205aa0d9.png)![Cornell University](https://www.signwell.com/assets/logos/cornell-logo-4058b5e20a38d8c14396b3c2125f886b95da4f723ee8c8989cf3de63236e1d7f.png)![ConvertFlow](https://www.signwell.com/assets/logos/convertflow-logo-7203f57cd6ac52aa18aa61d7f0c31153064dea056d2543d0b1a33a093ab6e40c.png)

![SignWell users avatars](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/avatars_line_bg-0f5f16a35e9db066744400b7abf75b609f006be04767192649dfef25fcc8fb9e.png)

![Embedded signatures demonstration](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/slider_demo-0d3cae33912d26a61ce3ab36f5fec19ad6ca179064931d859332ad10fa031ab0.png)

Embedded signatures

eSignature API


## How do embedded signatures work?

Instead of directing people to SignWell to sign your document, the optional embedded signing feature lets users
**fill out and sign it directly on your website or app.**

**Create a better user experience**
and reduce the risk of user churn in a multi-step, multi-site onboarding process: your platform, our signatures: quick and hassle-free.


[See embedded e-sign examples](https://www.signwell.com/api-demo/)

## What is an eSignature API?

The SignWell eSignature application programming interface (API) enables you to
**automate online signature requests.**
Integrate the API into your web or app code to create, fill out, or send documents for electronic signing.


Our eSignature API helps you quickly and securely request signatures and
**automate workflows,**
creating a simple, seamless signing experience.


[Try it for yourself!](https://www.signwell.com/resources/document-automation/)

## Build a Secure eSigning Workflow with a Few Lines of Code

The SignWell digital signature API is built around a clean, REST-based architecture. Here is how the four core workflow stages work and what they give your team.


### Create and Templatize

Use Text Tags to automatically place signature fields, dates, and initials exactly where they belong in any document. Or call a reusable Template via API to standardize compliance workflows across thousands of users without touching each file individually. The result is faster document prep and dramatically fewer manual errors.

### Embedded Signing (White-Label)

Use the Embedded Signing API to display documents in a secure iFrame or modal, keeping users inside your application throughout the entire signing process. Pair this with full white-label customization — your logo, your colors, your domain — so customers never feel like they have left your platform.

[See embedded eSign examples](https://www.signwell.com/api-demo/)

### Webhooks and Event Listeners

Configure webhooks to receive real-time event data as soon as a document is viewed, signed, or declined. Use these events to trigger your internal onboarding logic instantly, without polling or manual status checks.

### Audit and Archive

Retrieve a tamper-proof PDF and a comprehensive audit trail logging IP address, timestamp, and email for every document. Completed documents can be retrieved via API as a Base64-encoded file or accessed through a secure file URL for integration into your systems.

[Check out our API documentation to get started](https://developers.signwell.com/)

![](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/icons/stats/documents-d719a400666e4be2a8d8cbc812b5fea7ddef8c5d2d712319b90c86e291ff34d7.png)

10,000,000+ documents signed

![](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/icons/stats/uptime-bbc5c4fac3ebe63b55e20fd13bd8b9dd3e7eea62fb7ab5bdcad15c17328a5caf.png)

99.99% uptime reliability

![Capterra and G2](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/icons/stats/ratings-6fb6f6e2faaffaa0f7994fe0d247ea99dfa788072590cc1461533841053c3700.png)

4.9/5 on Capterra and 4.8/5 on G2

"I am so impressed with the SignWell API. In less than a day, I moved from never using SignWell to a fully functional automation powered by the API. I was able to get precisely the workflow that we needed."

![Brian Richards](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/testimonial_brian-f4fafaffda472ba4b9fcb303db86596af002abbfe02a91265905bf73ddb7c3ff.png)

Brian Richards

WPSessions

Once I reviewed the API documentation and tested it, I knew this was the right choice. I didn't have to deal with unnecessary complexity.

Their API did exactly what we needed and nothing more. This focus made the integration quick and painless.

Franklin Tarter - CTO, SunRaise Capital


SUCCESS STORIES

## Trusted by over 65,000 customers

Learn how organizations use SignWell's API to streamline their document workflows.


- [SunRaise Capital Elevates\\
with SignWell's API for Solar Financing](https://www.signwell.com/resources/customer-story/sunraise/)

- [Compliable\\
is Streamlining Compliance With SignWell's API](https://www.signwell.com/resources/customer-story/compliable/)

- [Offerwell\\
Transforms Real Estate Transactions with SignWell](https://www.signwell.com/resources/customer-story/offerwell/)


## SignWell Scales With You

### Pay-As-You-Go Pricing

Unlike legacy providers that lock you into expensive monthly contracts, SignWell scales with your actual usage. You only pay for the documents you send.


### Launch with Zero Overhead

Your first 25 documents every month are free. After that, pay a flat rate of $0.75 per document. No seat limits, no monthly minimums, and no hidden overage fees. If you do not send anything this month, you pay $0.


### Custom Plans for High-Scale Needs

For businesses sending high volumes of documents each month, SignWell offers custom enterprise plans built around your usage. Prepay for your API usage to save up to 40%.


[Contact Sales for High-Volume Pricing](https://www.signwell.com/pricing-contact/)

Solutions

## How the SignWell API can work for your business:

Whether you're signing contracts or embedding signatures into a website or app, the SignWell API can be customized to work for any industry:


### Software Development

You create it; we help you add eSignatures — the SignWell API makes it easy to build your ideal integration, no matter your skill level.

### Registrations & Onboarding

Effortlessly sign up new customers for your program, membership, or services — finalize onboarding so you can enjoy your growing community.

### Sales

Never miss a critical signature — easily automate document signing for your sales process or funnel to keep users flowing through your business experience.

### Health

The SignWell API prioritizes security and is HIPAA compliant — safely and securely fill out sensitive health or insurance documents directly on your website or app.

### Legal

From client intake forms to contracts, streamline your standard form documents with legally-binding automated signature requests so that you can stay concentrated on your business.

### Real Estate

Integrate signing with your existing real estate software or platform. Automate contracts to make signing user-friendly and straightforward, keeping things moving quickly.

![](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/businesses_served-14f513893c6222ab687e10cff8026fefbbcb781db8898890ae7963a30e44b2f7.png)

![](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/icons/ui/arrow-218fbc0a97b47ceea28e2eb331d2a62cd2af4c4b718d3af25854e108695383fa.png)

[Reach Out To Us!](https://www.signwell.com/pricing-contact/)

![](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/api_editor-daaee28f835b3d81d5bb5ead508c4140229ef5c6fefc38b5bef59dd1515ab5a8.png)

10,000,000
![](https://www.signwell.com/assets/api_landing/icons/plus_icon-cb88227ca1c32a837e03da7a13c003bf79c2033b2f8226289f6f678b46960830.svg)

documents signed and counting


## How SignWell API Powers Critical Workflows at Scale

### Eliminate Drop-off with White-Label Integration

**The challenge:** SaaS businesses that redirect users to a third-party site to sign documents risk losing them mid-onboarding.

**How SignWell solves it:** The Embedded Signing API keeps the entire experience within your platform.

**The outcome:** Customers sign contracts without ever leaving your product, resulting in higher completion rates and a smoother onboarding flow.

### Accelerate Revenue with Automated Workflows

**The challenge:** Manually chasing signatures via email slows deal cycles and creates administrative bottlenecks.

**How SignWell solves it:** When a deal stage changes in your CRM, your system can use the SignWell API to automatically generate and send contracts, with webhooks providing real-time status updates throughout the signing process.

**The outcome:** Sales documents are sent and filed instantly, eliminating missed revenue opportunities caused by slow paperwork.

### Secure Sensitive Data with HIPAA Compliance

**The challenge:** Health businesses that collect sensitive patient documents digitally face significant regulatory risk if the data is not properly secured.

**How SignWell solves it:** HIPAA and SOC 2 Type II compliant infrastructure encrypts data and provides a secure, audit-ready trail for every document.

**The outcome:** Patients can safely complete sensitive health forms directly on your portal, with full compliance and no paper.

### Zero-Touch Onboarding at Scale

**The challenge:** Manually sending individual waivers, NDAs, or membership agreements to thousands of new users is unscalable and error-prone.

**How SignWell solves it:** Bulk Send and Templates distribute standardized documents to any number of recipients simultaneously.

**The outcome:** Thousands of new members or employees are onboarded at once with zero administrative touchpoints.

### Standardize Compliance for Complex Deals

**The challenge:** In real estate, managing transactions with multiple signers often results in missing signatures or dates, leading to legal delays.

**How SignWell solves it:** Coordinates/text tags, along with automated workflows, enforce exactly where signatures, initials, and dates must be placed before a document can be submitted.

**The outcome:** Deals move quickly with user-friendly, legally binding contracts that are complete every time.

### Streamline Client Intake and Retainers

**The challenge:** Legal teams spend valuable billable hours manually preparing client intake forms and fee agreements.

**How SignWell solves it:** Automated signature requests integrated into practice management software trigger forms immediately after consultation.

**The outcome:** Lawyers focus on their cases while the API secures legally binding retainer agreements without any manual preparation.

## FAQ about the eSignature API

- What is the SignWell API?





The SignWell API lets you embed legally binding eSignatures into your product or service. It supports automated signature requests, embedded signing, and full workflow control using secure REST-based endpoints.

- Is the SignWell API secure and legally compliant?





Yes. The SignWell API is compliant with ESIGN, UETA, GDPR, and HIPAA. Documents signed through the API are legally binding and include a tamper-proof audit trail.

- Can I embed eSignatures directly on my website or in my app?





Yes. SignWell supports fully embedded signing experiences, allowing users to complete documents directly on your site or in your application—no redirects needed.

- How much does the API cost?





The API starts at $0.75 per document on a pay-as-you-go basis. The first 25 documents per month are free with a credit card on file. For high-volume needs, [custom pricing is available](https://www.signwell.com/pricing-contact/).

- Is there a free trial or sandbox environment for developers?





Yes. Developers can start with a free account and use up to 25 API requests per month (a credit card on file is required to prevent abuse). Our developer-friendly documentation makes it easy to get started quickly.

- What programming languages does the API support?





The SignWell API is REST-based and works with any language that supports HTTP requests, including JavaScript, Python, Ruby, PHP, Go, and more. [Our API documentation can be found here](https://developers.signwell.com/).

- Can I white-label the signing experience?





Yes. You can fully customize the signing experience to match your brand, including white-labeling the interface and automating templates and workflows.

- Does SignWell offer support for high-volume or enterprise use cases?





Yes. We offer dedicated support and custom pricing for high-volume or enterprise needs. [Contact our team](https://www.signwell.com/pricing-contact/) to learn more.

- Can I automate workflows with the SignWell API?





Absolutely. You can automate sending, signing, and tracking documents using our flexible API and reusable templates. Want to try it out? [Here are some examples](https://www.signwell.com/api-demo/).

- Is SignWell a good alternative to DocuSign or Adobe Sign?





Many customers switch to SignWell for its simplicity, lower cost, and developer-friendly API. It's a strong alternative for teams that want compliance and speed without the enterprise bloat. Learn more about how we compare to [DocuSign](https://www.signwell.com/docusign-alternative/) or [Adobe Sign](https://www.signwell.com/adobe-sign-alternative/).


![Need help image for SignWell](https://www.signwell.com/assets/homepage/need_help_img_2-2e7b0853cd99da7685b1878af13fd0c1ab67ae104b6c8c2d1de1a81e9076ddda.png)

## Need help setting up? We’re here to help

We’re available by email, phone, or over a Zoom call to support you whenever you need it.


[Reach out to us!](mailto:support@signwell.com)

Chat Widget

web.markdown:
# API and Zapier Requests

Getting Started with the SignWell API

The SignWell API provides a flexible solution for automating your document signing workflows.

To start with the SignWell API, go to the Settings -> API page and create your API key.

API documentation can be found here: [https://developers.signwell.com/](https://developers.signwell.com/)

API pricing: [https://www.signwell.com/api-pricing/](https://www.signwell.com/api-pricing/)

[Click here for API-related requests or high-volume API pricing](https://www.signwell.com/pricing-contact/).

## API Usage With Zapier

SignWell includes free unlimited usage with Zapier for all triggers:

- Document Send
- Document Viewed
- Document Completed

Sending documents through [Zapier](https://help.signwell.com/article/147-zapier-integration) with the Create Document From Template action is limited to 25 free API documents monthly. This applies to the Personal and Business plan (and requires an API plan for more than 25 signature requests a month):

![](https://d33v4339jhl8k0.cloudfront.net/docs/assets/54e2c53ae4b034c37ea8fb55/images/649c710db71911316e400050/file-IaCF9Ldy4i.jpg)

An API plan is only needed if you go past the free limit of signature requests that we give each plan:

- Free plan - 3 free signature requests per month
- All other plans - 25 free signature requests per month

Because our API/Zapier credits pricing is based on usage, you will only pay for what you use.

If you have reached your monthly limit with these Zapier signature requests, you can [see pricing to add an API plan to your account (or get more information)](https://www.signwell.com/api-pricing/).


Please don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions or need help with the API at [support@signwell.com](mailto:support@signwell.com).

There are no hidden costs or contracts. You can cancel at any time, and there is no penalty for doing so.

Did this answer your question?Thanks for the feedbackThere was a problem submitting your feedback. Please try again later.

YesNo

Still need help?
[Contact Us](https://help.signwell.com/article/251-api-and-zapier-requests#) [Contact Us](https://help.signwell.com/article/251-api-and-zapier-requests#)

Last updated on July 23, 2025

### Related Articles

- [Integrate SignWell With Thousands of Other Apps (Through Zapier)](https://help.signwell.com/article/273-integrate-signwell-with-thousands-of-other-apps-through-zapier)

# is signwell free

![](https://static.esignglobal.com/6deccd6f_f421_5f11_ac14_52f10c173fee_8_19_3fc66c6c73.png)

Shunfang


![](https://static.esignglobal.com/6deccd6f_f421_5f11_ac14_52f10c173fee_8_19_3fc66c6c73.png)

2025-12-01


![](https://static.esignglobal.com/6deccd6f_f421_5f11_ac14_52f10c173fee_8_19_3fc66c6c73.png)

3min


![Twitter](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/twitter.svg)![Facebook](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/facebook.svg)![Linkedin](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/link.svg)

## Is SignWell Free? A Business Perspective on Electronic Signature Costs

In the competitive landscape of electronic signature solutions, businesses often seek cost-effective tools to streamline document workflows without compromising compliance or functionality. SignWell, a popular e-signature platform, frequently raises questions about its accessibility, particularly whether it offers a truly free tier. From a commercial standpoint, understanding SignWell's pricing model is crucial for small teams, startups, and enterprises evaluating options amid rising operational costs. This article delves into SignWell's free offerings, limitations, and broader implications, while comparing it to key competitors like DocuSign, Adobe Sign, and eSignGlobal.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251022_1636_simple_compose_01k85gx9g0fxybwyekzqatj7vw_98c55ccdc2.png)

### Exploring SignWell's Free Plan: What You Get and What It Costs

SignWell positions itself as an affordable alternative in the e-signature market, emphasizing simplicity for individuals and small businesses. At its core, the question "Is SignWell free?" has a nuanced answer: yes, it offers a free plan, but with significant limitations that may not suit all commercial needs.

The free tier allows users to sign and send unlimited documents, which sounds appealing on the surface. However, every document sent includes a SignWell branding watermark, and recipients must create a free account to sign, potentially creating friction in professional workflows. Storage is capped at 5 active documents at any time, with no access to advanced features like custom branding, API integrations, or bulk sending. From a business observation, this plan works well for occasional personal use—such as freelancers signing contracts sporadically—but falls short for teams handling high volumes. For instance, a small consulting firm might find the watermark unprofessional for client-facing agreements, pushing them toward paid upgrades.

Paid plans start at $8 per month for the Basic tier (billed annually), removing watermarks and increasing storage to 20 documents, while adding templates and reminders. The Pro plan at $15 per month unlocks unlimited storage, custom fields, and team collaboration. Enterprise options are custom-priced for larger organizations needing SSO or advanced reporting. In essence, while SignWell's free plan provides entry-level access, it's more of a trial mechanism than a sustainable free solution for business operations. Companies relying on it long-term risk scalability issues, as evidenced by user reviews highlighting the need to upgrade quickly for compliance-heavy industries like real estate or legal services.

### Legal Considerations for Electronic Signatures in the US and Beyond

Since SignWell is a US-based platform, its free and paid plans operate under the Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act (ESIGN) and the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA), which grant electronic signatures the same legal validity as wet-ink signatures in the United States for most transactions. This means documents signed via SignWell's free plan are enforceable in US courts, provided they meet basic requirements like intent to sign and record retention. However, businesses operating internationally must consider regional variances. For example, in the European Union, the eIDAS regulation imposes stricter standards for qualified electronic signatures, which SignWell supports in paid tiers but not fully in the free version due to limited audit trails.

From a commercial lens, this regulatory alignment makes SignWell's free option viable for domestic US startups testing the waters. Yet, for cross-border dealings—say, a US firm contracting with EU partners—the free plan's lack of robust verification could expose risks, such as disputes over authenticity. In Asia-Pacific markets, where data privacy laws like Singapore's PDPA or Hong Kong's PDPO apply, SignWell complies minimally but lacks native integrations that regional alternatives offer, potentially increasing compliance costs indirectly.

### SignWell's Free Limitations in a Business Context

Delving deeper, the free plan's constraints become evident in real-world applications. Unlimited signing sounds generous, but the 5-document storage limit forces frequent deletions or upgrades, disrupting workflows. There's no support for mobile apps in the free tier, and features like payment collection or conditional routing are absent, limiting its use for e-commerce integrations. Businesses observing market trends note that while SignWell saves upfront costs, hidden inefficiencies—like manual follow-ups due to no automated reminders—can inflate operational expenses over time.

Comparatively, in a sector where 80% of agreements are now digital (per recent industry reports), free tools like SignWell appeal to bootstrapped ventures. However, for mid-sized firms, the transition from free to paid often reveals scalability gaps. User feedback on platforms like G2 indicates satisfaction with ease of use but frustration with growth barriers, underscoring why many evaluate paid competitors after initial trials.

### Comparing SignWell to Leading Competitors

To provide a balanced commercial overview, it's essential to benchmark SignWell against established players. The e-signature market is dominated by DocuSign and Adobe Sign for enterprise needs, while regional options like eSignGlobal cater to global compliance. Other notables include HelloSign (now Dropbox Sign) and PandaDoc, which blend signing with document management. Below is a markdown comparison table highlighting key aspects, based on 2025 pricing and features (annual billing where applicable). This analysis remains neutral, focusing on value for different business scales.

| Feature/Plan | SignWell (Free/Basic) | DocuSign (Personal/Standard) | Adobe Sign (Individual/Teams) | eSignGlobal (Essential/Pro) | HelloSign (Essentials) | PandaDoc (Essentials) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Starting Price** | Free / $96/year | $120/year / $300/user/year | $10/month / $25/user/month | $199/year / Custom | $15/month | $19/user/month |
| **Documents/Envelopes** | Unlimited send (5 storage) / 20 storage | 5/month / ~100/year | Unlimited / Unlimited | 100/month / Unlimited | 3/month / Unlimited | Unlimited templates |
| **Users/Seats** | 1 / Unlimited team | 1 / Multi-user | 1 / Multi-user | Unlimited / Unlimited | 1 / Multi-user | Multi-user |
| **Compliance** | US ESIGN/UETA; basic global | Global (eIDAS, ESIGN); advanced audit | eIDAS, ESIGN; Acrobat integration | 100+ countries; APAC native (PDPA, PDPO) | ESIGN; basic EU | ESIGN; CRM focus |
| **Key Features** | Basic signing; watermarks on free | Templates, reminders, bulk send | PDF editing, payments | Access code verification; Singpass/IAm Smart integration | Simple UI; reminders | Proposals, analytics |
| **API/Integrations** | Limited on free | Robust (Starter $600/year) | Strong (Salesforce, Microsoft) | Flexible API; regional focus | Basic Zapier | Advanced CRM |
| **Best For** | Individuals/small trials | Teams/enterprises | Creative/legal pros | APAC/global compliance | Quick personal use | Sales teams |
| **Drawbacks** | Watermarks, no advanced verification | High cost for add-ons | Steep learning curve | Less brand recognition | Limited free tier | Overkill for simple signing |

This table illustrates SignWell's edge in entry-level accessibility but highlights competitors' strengths in scalability and compliance. DocuSign excels in enterprise automation, though at a premium; Adobe Sign integrates seamlessly with creative tools; eSignGlobal stands out for regional affordability without sacrificing global reach.

### DocuSign: The Enterprise Standard

DocuSign remains the gold standard for electronic signatures, powering workflows for Fortune 500 companies with its comprehensive suite. Pricing starts at $120 annually for Personal (5 envelopes/month) and scales to $300/user/year for Standard, including templates and reminders. Business Pro at $480/user/year adds bulk send and payments, ideal for high-volume operations. However, API access requires separate plans from $600/year, and APAC users face latency and compliance surcharges. Commercially, it's reliable but costly for smaller firms.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1927_Docusign_simple_compose_01kb2gzcxjf90s2d849a4yzkes_f4a40eeee0.png)

### Adobe Sign: Integrated Document Powerhouse

Adobe Sign leverages the Adobe ecosystem for seamless PDF handling, starting at $10/month for individuals with unlimited signing and basic compliance. Teams plans at $25/user/month add collaboration and integrations with Acrobat. It's particularly strong for industries needing editable forms, but lacks native APAC optimizations, and advanced features like identity verification incur extra fees. Businesses value its polish, though the interface can overwhelm non-tech users.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1941_Adobe_simple_compose_01kb2httnmftqttna4mq0d3mrb_3366684d27.png)

### eSignGlobal: A Compliant Global Contender

eSignGlobal emerges as a versatile option, compliant in over 100 mainstream countries and regions, with particular advantages in the Asia-Pacific. It supports native regulations like Hong Kong's PDPO and Singapore's PDPA, offering seamless integrations with local systems such as Hong Kong's IAm Smart and Singapore's Singpass for enhanced verification. Pricing is competitive; the Essential plan costs just $16.6/month (or $199/year), allowing up to 100 documents for signature, unlimited user seats, and access code verification—delivering high value on compliance without the premiums of global giants. For detailed plans, visit [eSignGlobal's pricing page](https://www.esignglobal.com/pricing). In business terms, it's cost-effective for cross-border teams, though it may require evaluation for ultra-complex enterprise needs.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/Im_smart_cc2801eb55.png)

### Other Competitors: HelloSign and PandaDoc

HelloSign (Dropbox Sign) offers a straightforward free tier with 3 documents/month, scaling to $15/month for unlimited use—great for quick, no-frills signing but limited in analytics. PandaDoc, at $19/user/month, focuses on sales proposals with embedded signing, appealing to marketing teams but less ideal for pure legal workflows.

### Final Thoughts: Choosing the Right Fit

In summary, SignWell's free plan provides a low-barrier entry but demands upgrades for serious business use. For DocuSign alternatives emphasizing regional compliance, eSignGlobal offers a balanced, cost-effective choice. Evaluate based on your scale, geography, and needs to optimize ROI in the evolving e-signature market.

FAQs

Is SignWell completely free to use?

SignWell offers a free plan that allows users to send up to three documents per month with basic eSignature features. However, it is not entirely free for unlimited use or advanced functionalities, which require a paid subscription.

What features are included in SignWell's free plan?

The free plan of SignWell includes core eSignature capabilities such as document uploading, electronic signing, and basic tracking. Limitations include a cap of three documents per month and no access to premium features like custom branding or API integration.

How can users upgrade from SignWell's free plan?

Users can upgrade to a paid plan through their SignWell account dashboard. Paid plans provide unlimited documents, advanced security options, and additional workflow tools, with pricing tiers based on usage needs.
